metadata.description: Sandbox marketplace for testing the test-case-generator plugin

plugins.name: input-analyzer
plugins.description: Multi-lens feature analyzer: ingests heterogeneous sources (specs, OpenAPI, UI docs, code, compliance docs) and emits per-lens Claude Code SKILL.md files (functional / technical / ui / nfr / glossary) inside a 6-layer System → Business Domain → Sub-Domain → Feature → User Story → Use Case tree. Use standalone for system audits, modernization scoping, monolith decomposition, onboarding documentation, or as the upstream input for the test-case-generator plugin. Anchors all classification to the companion `input-hierarchization` plugin (canonical vocabulary + tree) and validates L2 Requirement statements with the companion `ears` plugin — install both alongside.

plugins.name: test-case-generator
plugins.description: Multi-strategy test case generator: reads per-lens SKILL.md files (produced by the input-analyzer plugin) and dispatches 5 parallel testing strategies (Component, Integration, Edge Case, Limit Case, Cross Case) to produce domain-organized, tagged Markdown test scenario documents.

plugins.name: tet-atf-knowledge
plugins.description: Read-only knowledge bundle for the TET-ATF (Test Enablement Team Automated Test Framework) skeleton. Installs 14 context-activated skills covering framework architecture, layer rules, tag system, utils catalog, coding standards, API patterns, CI/CD, and more — so Claude knows all conventions and patterns when working inside any TET-ATF-based test repository.

plugins.name: ears
plugins.description: Read-only knowledge bundle for the Easy Approach to Requirements Syntax (EARS) — five natural-language patterns (Ubiquitous, State-driven, Event-driven, Optional Feature, Unwanted Behaviour) plus a Complex composite that constrain natural language just enough to make requirements clear, concise, unambiguous, and testable. Activates whenever an agent must author, review, classify, or rewrite a requirement statement. Authoring convention referenced by the input-hierarchization plugin's L2 Requirement layer.

plugins.name: input-hierarchization
plugins.description: Read-only knowledge bundle defining the canonical specification vocabulary (Functional Domain, Capability, Asset, Dataset, Feature, Process, Activity, Task, Golden Data) and the 6-level system hierarchization tree (Domain → Requirement → Process → Step → Feature → Use Case / Acceptance Criteria), with EARS and BPMN authoring conventions. Activates whenever an agent must classify a piece of input data or organize a heterogeneous input list into a coherent system tree.

plugins.name: flow-logger
plugins.description: Real-time observability — logs agent/tool events to JSONL and serves a live dashboard with flow tree, token tracking, and dual themes (Classic + Cyber).
